name: Carribean-Style Seafood Chowder
recipeIngredient: 2 lb assorted firm-fleshed fish (snapper, drum), reserve bones and shrimp shells
1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut fish into 2-inch cubes. Toss with shrimp, lime juice, and jalapenos. Refrigerate for 2 hours.
2. Place fish bones and shrimp shells in a stockpot with one unpeeled onion quartered, one unpeeled garlic clove quartered, and one celery rib. Cover with water and simmer for 1 hour. Drain and discard solids. Reserve the stock.
3. Brown bacon in a skillet. Remove bacon and set aside. In the same skillet, sauté remaining onions, celery ribs, and peppers until tender and golden. Add garlic and cook for an additional minute.
4. Add potatoes, thyme, bay leaves, and nutmeg to the hot stock. Add tomatoes and tomato paste (if used). Cook until potatoes are barely tender, about 15-20 minutes.
5. Return bacon to the pot. Add fish and shrimp along with their marinade. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes until cooked through.
6. Thin with beer if needed, and adjust heat level to your preference.
